stem: Which of the following is equivalent to $(2x - 3)^2$?
explanation:
Expand $(2x - 3)^2$ by writing it as $(2x - 3)(2x - 3)$ and using FOIL:

First: $(2x)(2x) = 4x^2$
Outer: $(2x)(-3) = -6x$
Inner: $(-3)(2x) = -6x$
Last: $(-3)(-3) = +9$

Combining all terms: $4x^2 - 6x - 6x + 9$
Combining the $x$ terms: $-6x + (-6x) = -12x$

The equivalent expression is $4x^2 - 12x + 9$.

distractorRationale.A: Incomplete expansion: the student only squared the first and last terms of the binomial ($4x^2$ and $9$), forgetting the middle cross-term $-12x$ entirely.
distractorRationale.B: Sign error on the constant: the student correctly computed the $4x^2$ and $-12x$ terms but treated the last term as $(-3)(+3) = -9$ instead of $(-3)(-3) = +9$.
distractorRationale.C: Coefficient error: the student forgot to square the coefficient 2 on the first term, writing $2x^2$ instead of $(2x)^2 = 4x^2$.
